items.headline: Trump says Israel, Lebanon leaders to hold talks
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump has said that leaders of Israel and Lebanon will speak on April 16, as Washington pushes to ease hostilities after the rivals' first direct talks in decades.

items.headline: US optimistic about reaching peace deal with Iran
items.summary: The United States is discussing a possible second round of peace talks with Iran in Pakistan and is optimistic about reaching a deal, U.S. officials said Wednesday, as Tehran threatened to shut down Red Sea trade unless Washington lifted a naval blockade of i…

items.headline: U.S. and Iran Report Progress on Ceasefire Framework as Naval Blockade Continues
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump stated on Wednesday, April 15, that the war with Iran is “very close to over,” citing U.S. military dominance. “We’ve beaten them militarily, totally,” Trump told Fox Business in a prerecorded interview, adding, “I think it’s close…

items.headline: Trump says Israel and Lebanon leaders will hold talks today
items.summary: Meanwhile, Pakistan’s army chief is set to meet with Iranian officials in Tehran in a bid to arrange a second round of negotiations between the US and Iran.

items.headline: Iran-Linked Ships Take New Path To Trickle Into the Persian Gulf
items.summary: At least two US-sanctioned, Iran-linked vessels made their way through the Strait of Hormuz and into the Persian Gulf on Thursday, taking what may be a new route from the United Arab Emirates even as a US blockade enters its third day.

items.headline: Live updates: Optimism grows over US-Iran peace talks; Europe faces higher gas prices ‘for years’

items.headline: How US blockade on Iran sanctioned ships turning around

items.headline: US-sanctioned supertankers enter Gulf despite blockade
items.summary: A second U.S.-sanctioned supertanker has entered the Strait of Hormuz despite Washington’s blockade on vessels linked to Iranian ports, raising questions over enforcement. The move comes amid stalled U.S.-Iran talks, with Iran continuing crude exports near re…

items.headline: GOP holds with Trump on Iran war, but cracks emerge as deadline nears
items.summary: Senate Republicans block a fourth war powers resolution on Trump's Iran war, but questions grow as the 60-day congressional deadline approaches.

items.headline: North Korea showing 'serious ability' over nuclear weapon
items.summary: North Korea is showing a "very serious increase" in its ability to produce nuclear weapons, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency has said.

items.headline: Iran war diverts U.S. military and attention from Asia ahead of Trump's summit with China's leader

items.headline: In 2011, Barack Obama said it was time to ‘pivot’ to Asia. But 15 years later, the U.S. is still at war in the Middle East
items.summary: “Rebalancing to Asia is highly relevant to America’s national interests, but it has been undercut by many bad decisions.”

items.headline: Trump squeezed between Israel and Turkey as Netanyahu, Erdogan escalate feud
items.summary: Israel-Turkey tensions escalate sharply as Netanyahu and Erdogan clash over Gaza, Iran and regional influence, creating a diplomatic challenge for Trump.

items.headline: S&P 500 hits new all-time high as investors shrug off Iran war oil price spike
items.summary: The S&P 500 hit a new all-time high Wednesday, a remarkable turnaround for stocks while the war with Iran drives up energy prices and cuts into global growth.

items.headline: Why BLTs and salad just got more expensive — tariffs, war send tomato prices soaring
items.summary: Tomato prices have risen sharply in recent months, driven largely by trade policy, economists said.

items.headline: Saudi Pipeline Attack Removes 700,000 Barrels Daily from Oil Exports
items.summary: The Saudi energy ministry confirmed on Thursday that Iranian strikes on the kingdom's East-West pipeline have reduced its export capacity by approximately 700,000 barrels per day (bpd). The ministry stated the attacks hit a pumping station on the vital pipeli…
